sections.heading: This Act may be cited as the Local Authorities Short title
sections.text:
Superannuation Fund (Amendment) Act, 2015, and shall be read
as one with the Local Authorities Superannuation Fund Act, in this Cap. 284
Act referred to as the principal Act.

sections.heading: Section two of the principal Act is amended by the deletion Amendment
Section nineteen of the principal Act is amended by the Amendment
sections.text:
deletion of subsection (1) and the substitution therefor of the of section 19
following:
(1) A member shall contribute to the fund at the rate
of ten percent of the member’s pensionable emoluments
except that the Minister may, in consultation with the Fund
and based on an actuarial valuation undertaken to determine
the financial sustainability of the Fund, by statutory
instrument prescribe a lower or higher rate of contributions.

sections.heading: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section twenty- Repeal and
sections.text:
five and the substitution therefor of the following: replacement
of section 25

sections.heading: (1) A member shall be paid a retirement benefit Retirement
sections.text:
in the form of an annuity at the rate of one seven hundred benefit
and twentieth of the retiring pensionable emoluments of
the member for each completed month of the member’s
continuous service.
Single copies of this Act may be obtained from the Government Printer,
P.O. Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka, Price K 4.00 each.
Local Authorities Superannuation
150 No. 8 of 2015 ] Fund (Amendment)
(2) A member may commute one-third or two-thirds
of the member’s retirement benefit for lump sum at the
date of the member’s retirement—
(a) if the member is retired under section twenty-
seven, at the rate of twenty-eight ngwee for
each one ngwee of annuity commuted; or
(b) if the member retires or is retired under section
twenty-six, twenty-eight or twenty-nine, at the
rate laid down for the member’s age at the date
of the member’s retirement in the appropriate
Schedules.
(3) Notwithstanding subsection (1) and (2), the
Minister may, in consultation with the Fund and based on
an actuarial valuation undertaken to determine the financial
sustainability of the Fund, by statutory instrument prescribe
the rate at which a retirement benefit is payable.
Repeal and 5. The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section twenty-
replacement six and the substitution therefor of the following:
of section 26
Retirement 26. (1) Subject to subsection (2), a member shall retire
due to age
on attaining the pensionable age.
(2) A member may retire on attaining the age of —
(a) fifty-five years if, twelve months before attaining
that age, the member notifies the contributing
employer of the member’s intention to retire at
that age and the employer approves the
retirement; or
(b) sixty-five years if, twelve months before attaining
the pensionable age, the member notifies the
contributing employer of the member’s intention
to retire at the age of sixty-five years and the
employer approves the retirement.
(3) A member who retires in accordance with
subsection (1) or (2) shall be paid a retirement benefit.
Amendment 6. Section forty-one of the Principal Act is amended by the—
of section 41
(a) insertion, immediately after paragraph (g), of the following
new paragraph:
Local Authorities Superannuation
Fund (Amendment) [No. of 2015 151
(h) providing for arrangements to ensure the
protection of accrued pension rights as provided
for in the Constitution and;
(b) renumbering of paragraph (h) as paragraph (i).

sections.heading: The principal Act is amended by the deletion of the words General
sections.text:
“pension age” wherever they appear and the substitution therefor amendment
of the words “ pensionable age ”.
152 No. 8 of 2015 ]
